meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing what to build. Learn a proven client workflow to validate SaaS ideas, execute with Antigravity and Claude Code, and ship your first MVP fast.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start solving 'workflows.' By interviewing potential clients about their most painful manual spreadsheet or repetitive task, you uncover profitable SaaS opportunities. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to iterate the logic, turning a client's frustration into your recurring revenue.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank screen, waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never hits. The graveyard of abandoned SaaS projects is filled with founders who built what they wanted instead of what the market desperately needs. The silence isn't a lack of creativity; it is a lack of customer proximity.

Real wealth in the AI era isn't built on novel ideas—it's built on boring, broken workflows. When you transition from 'idea generation' to 'workflow auditing,' you stop being a dreamer and start being a solution architect. This checklist provides the exact framework to identify profitable pain points and build high-value tools that clients will pay for immediately.

steps.title: Conduct a Workflow Audit
steps.description: Identify a niche industry and interview 5 people about their 'daily spreadsheet hell.' Look for tasks that are repetitive, error-prone, and currently managed via email or Excel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS Product Manager. Create a survey script to identify high-friction manual workflows in small business operations, focusing on time-to-value and willingness to pay.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a CSV template for tracking workflow pain points from customer interviews.'

steps.title: Define the Minimal Viable Workflow
steps.description: Map the 'Happy Path' of the workflow. You aren't building a platform; you are building an automation layer that eliminates the most painful step of their current process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a lean MVP architecture for an AI-powered automation tool that replaces [Workflow Name]. Prioritize core functionality over UI/UX.

steps.title: Architect the Logic with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the backend logic. Focus on the integration between the client's data source and the AI processing layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Python backend using FastAPI that accepts CSV/JSON input, processes it via an LLM to extract key insights, and outputs a structured report.
steps.claude_code_command: claude code --write 'Implement the FastAPI endpoint for processing client workflow data.'

steps.title: Iterative Development with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly turn the scaffold into a working prototype. Focus on speed-to-market—if it works in the CLI, it's ready for a beta test.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite for the core processing logic to ensure accuracy and handle edge cases in client data inputs.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment and get it into the hands of your interviewees. Their feedback is the only metric that matters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration file for a cloud provider that supports auto-scaling for high-frequency API requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'git push origin main && vercel --prod'

pro_tips: Always build the 'Export to CSV' feature first; it validates that the user can take your data back to their old, comfortable workflow.
Ignore authentication for the first 10 users; use hardcoded API keys or simple URL-based access to reduce friction during validation.
Record your screen while the client uses your tool; watch where they hesitate, as that is where your product is failing them.
Charge a nominal fee from day one, even if it's $5. If they won't pay $5, they won't pay $500, and you shouldn't be building it.
Don't build a dashboard until you have at least 3 paying customers; focus exclusively on the 'Action' button that solves the problem.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, reach out to your initial interviewees and offer them 30 days of free access in exchange for a video testimonial. Use that social proof to acquire your next 10 paid users.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the perfect solution for a real business problem. The market is waiting for your expertise to remove their daily bottlenecks. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
